oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: raw command/string or JSON value as specified by public_test_cases) -> stdout exactly matching expected_output

trigger_keywords: how do I pass arguments to a filter?
what syntax separates the filter name from args?
does a colon work as a separator?
how do I specify extra parameters
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The argument separator is ':' and empty strings passed after it are treated as boolean false.

trigger_keywords: what is the loop index starting at?
does the index start at zero?
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The loop index variable 'forloop.index' is initialized to the integer 1.

trigger_keywords: is true the fallback for rethrow?
how to report parse errors directly
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Setting the configuration variable 'rethrow_errors' to the boolean 'true' causes actual parse errors to be reported.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_partial_inclusion, feature11_html_table_rows, feature12_whitespace_trimming, feature13_error_normalization, feature14_safe_member_exposure, feature1_tokenization, feature2_literal_and_comment_rendering, feature3_variable_resolution, feature4_custom_filter_pipeline, feature5_builtin_text_filters, feature6_numeric_and_sequence_filters, feature7_conditional_rendering, feature8_loop_rendering, feature9_assignment_rendering. Feature descriptions include: Render named partial templates with explicit objects, repeated collections, local variables, nested partials, and dynamically selected partial names.; Render table-row loops as HTML table row and cell fragments with row/column classes, counters, offset, and limit.; Trim leading and trailing whitespace around control tags when trim markers are used.; Normalize parse, render, include-recursion, and missing-partial errors into language-neutral stdout categories.; Expose explicitly permitted object members and a string transformer while hiding unrenderable objects by default.; Split template text into literal, output, and block tokens without rendering it.; Render plain text exactly and omit comment block contents while preserving surrounding literal text.; Resolve scalar values, nested object paths, and missing values in output expressions.; Apply caller-supplied text filters, passing the current value and literal or variable arguments through the pipeline.; Transform strings and arrays with built-in filters for escaping, truncation, joining, splitting, replacing, and newline handling.; Perform arithmetic, size, first/last, sorting, and property projection operations in rendered expressions.; Render branches based on truthiness, boolean operators, comparisons, nested conditions, and inverse conditions.; Iterate over arrays and ranges, expose loop counters, honor limits and offsets, and support nested loops plus break/continue control.; Assign values from variables, literals, and filtered expressions so later output expressions can read them.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: template=[' {% for a in b %} ... ', ' {% if 1 =! 2 %}ok{% endif %} ', " {{ car.gm | add_smiley : ':-(' | add_smiley : ':-(' }} ", ' {{ car.gm | cite_funny }} ', ' {{best_cars}} ', ' {{car.bmw}} {{car.gm}} {{car.bmw}} ', 'foo\n\t  {%- if true %}hi tobi{% endif %}', 'foo{% comment %} comment {% endcomment %}bar', 'this text should come out of the template without change...', '{% assign foo = values %}.{{ foo[0] }}.', '{% assign foo = values %}.{{ foo[1] }}.', "{% if a == 'and' and b == 'or' and c == 'foo and bar' and d == 'bar or baz' %}YE"], data=['{dict}'], partials=['{dict}'], rethrow_errors=['True'], object=['{dict}'], <raw>=[' {{funk}} {{so}} {{brother}} ', '5', 'hello world']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the range syntax pattern used in the 'feature3_variable_resolution' block
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The '..' sequence generates a consecutive integer sequence from the lower bound to the exclusive upper bound.

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the same sequence restriction logic as found in constraint C014
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The '..' syntax creates a sequence of values where the end value is inclusive, unlike the exclusive upper bound of C014.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_partial_inclusion.json.description: Render named partial templates with explicit objects, repeated collections, local variables, nested partials, and dynamically selected partial names.

feature11_html_table_rows.json.description: Render table-row loops as HTML table row and cell fragments with row/column classes, counters, offset, and limit.

input.template: {% tablerow n in numbers cols:3%} {{n}} {% endtablerow %}

cases.expected_output: 1 2 3 4 5 6

input.template: {% tablerow n in numbers cols:2%}{{tablerowloop.col}}{% endtablerow %}

cases.expected_output: 12 12 12

feature12_whitespace_trimming.json.description: Trim leading and trailing whitespace around control tags when trim markers are used.

feature13_error_normalization.json.description: Normalize parse, render, include-recursion, and missing-partial errors into language-neutral stdout categories.

feature14_safe_member_exposure.json.description: Expose explicitly permitted object members and a string transformer while hiding unrenderable objects by default.

feature1_tokenization.json.description: Split template text into literal, output, and block tokens without rendering it.

feature2_literal_and_comment_rendering.j.description: Render plain text exactly and omit comment block contents while preserving surrounding literal text.

input.template: this text should come out of the template without change...

cases.expected_output: this text should come out of the template without change...

feature3_variable_resolution.json.description: Resolve scalar values, nested object paths, and missing values in output expressions.

feature4_custom_filter_pipeline.json.description: Apply caller-supplied text filters, passing the current value and literal or variable arguments through the pipeline.

feature5_builtin_text_filters.json.description: Transform strings and arrays with built-in filters for escaping, truncation, joining, splitting, replacing, and newline handling.

feature6_numeric_and_sequence_filters.js.description: Perform arithmetic, size, first/last, sorting, and property projection operations in rendered expressions.

feature7_conditional_rendering.json.description: Render branches based on truthiness, boolean operators, comparisons, nested conditions, and inverse conditions.

input.template: {% if a or b or c %}YES{% endif %}
{% if a == 'and' and b == 'or' and c == 'foo and bar' and d == 'bar or baz' %}YES{% endif %}
{% if true %}{% if false %}NO{% else %}YES{% endif %}{% else %}NO{% endif %}

feature8_loop_rendering.json.description: Iterate over arrays and ranges, expose loop counters, honor limits and offsets, and support nested loops plus break/continue control.

input.template: {%for i in array limit:4 offset:2 %}{{ i }}{%endfor%}

feature9_assignment_rendering.json.description: Assign values from variables, literals, and filtered expressions so later output expressions can read them.

_constraints.input_condition: template provided without data object; no partials; no action specified
_constraints.output_rule: Raw template text split into tokens on newlines; tokens are literal strings preserving original whitespace around tags
Block tags are emitted as separate tokens with surrounding whitespace preserved as newlines in the output stream of tokens

_constraints.input_condition: template provided with data object; no partials; comment blocks used
_constraints.output_rule: Comment block content between {% comment %} and {% endcomment %} is stripped out; surrounding literal text is preserved

_constraints.input_condition: template contains variable reference (e.g. {{var}}); data object provided
_constraints.output_rule: Variable resolves to data.value or data.subobj.key; missing values render as empty string
False renders as empty string

_constraints.input_condition: filter chain in template without data; custom filter name provided
_constraints.output_rule: Custom filter receives value and arguments (literal or variable); chains sequentially

_constraints.input_condition: custom filter with ':' separator for arguments
_constraints.output_rule: Filter name and arguments split by colon, arguments passed to filter function
Filter transforms string/array; arguments provided after colon
Special characters like < HTML escaped to <
String truncated to char count; result ends with ...
String truncated to word count; result ends with ...
Arithmetic performed on operand and argument
Integer division used
Sequence generated for range
Branches evaluated based on hardcoded values
Expression evaluated using data; false values render falsy branches
String comparison performed
Inner evaluated based on outer's falsiness
Only one branch rendered
Each item rendered consecutively
Seq renovated to range of values inclusive
forloop.index starts at 1; forloop.length shows total items
Slice array based on offset and limit values

_constraints.input_condition: assign block with name and value
_constraints.output_rule: Variable name bound to value; reference later
Array reference used later (e.g. [0])
Named partial rendered with data object
Partial rendered passing value from data[0] to named argument

_constraints.input_condition: include statement with variable data array
_constraints.output_rule: Partial loop for each element of data array
Rendered global template with local variables merged
HTML table row fragments with cols class per row
Each row outputs designated colspan and nested column classes
Leading/trailing whitespace removed around control tag

_constraints.input_condition: trimmed block markers used inside block
_constraints.output_rule: Whitespace removed but not between literals, only around tags
Normalized error message printed to stdout
Actual parse error reported
Specific Liquid-like error displayed
Member value rendered

_constraints.input_condition: safe includes partials or data object
_constraints.output_rule: Partial data object used to satisfy member access
